svg.title: openclaw-dev — architecture at a glance
svg.desc: openclaw-dev runs on Azure Container Apps and talks to Azure OpenAI in Microsoft Foundry using a passwordless Managed Identity token. Users reach it from Microsoft Teams (web, desktop, mobile) or any browser. One command — devclaw up — provisions everything from Bicep.

g.text: Your own AI chat in Microsoft Teams — powered by Azure OpenAI in Microsoft Foundry. One command to deploy.
Chat with the bot from anywhere.
Open the link, sign in once.
Deploy from your laptop in one command.
Why this is safe — in plain English
on Azure Container Apps · scales automatically
Isolated, ephemeral container — OpenClaw runs arbitrary code, so never on your laptop.
No passwords, no API keys — Managed Identity signs every call to Foundry.
The model your app talks to.
direct HTTP — no SDK in the way
— default. The latest, most powerful OpenAI models (gpt-5.5) are available too.
devclaw up — a thin wrapper around the Azure Developer CLI (azd).
One command provisions everything on this diagram. Infrastructure defined in Bicep. Idempotent — run it again to roll forward.

svg.text: Built with Microsoft Entra ID · Managed Identity · Azure Container Apps · Microsoft Foundry · Bicep
